summary: News articles published within the previous 24 hours have outlined several specific developments in small UAS activity and technology. Ukraine's Ptashka drone set new strike quadcopter records by flying on a glass thread undetectable by Russian forces, while a Ukrainian firm unveiled the Chipa net gun capable of intercepting FPV drones at 25 metres. The T-80BVM tank survived 18 Ukrainian FPV drone strikes, and Russian forces tested a "penguin" camouflage suit in snowy terrain, only to have at least two soldiers neutralized by FPV drones within hours. The Pentagon named the Shrike Fiber drone as a top winner in its "Drone Dominance" competition, and Ukraine's CD-T10 radar system was reported to detect FPV drones and guided aerial bombs within a 15 km radius. Additionally, the U.S. Army National Guard purchased small FPV strike drones, and Ukraine's BlueBird introduced an updated Chipa net gun model.
